methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Slip Robotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by John Jakomin, having raised $44M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage, including peer companies like 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using the $44M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage step-ups, implies a valuation of $84M. The peer-median method evaluated six same-sector, same-stage comparables, which span a range from $300M to $10.0B and yield a median valuation of $1.1B. This wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in the peer set—1X Technologies commands a $10.0B valuation while Fairmat trades at $350M—suggesting differentiation in capital efficiency, technology maturity, or market positioning.

The triangulated valuation range is $71M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $579M. The gulf between the stage-progression implied value and the peer median reflects uncertainty about where Slip Robotics falls within the competitive distribution. Key uncertainties include the company's relative progress on core technical milestones, revenue traction if any, and whether peer valuations reflect durable market positions or speculative premia in frontier robotics.
